B.title: Section B — Short Answer I (2 marks each)

questions.q: Find ∛729 using prime factorisation.
Is 500 a perfect cube? Give reason.
questions.ans: 500 = 2²×5³. Factor 2 appears only twice, not in a complete triple. So 500 is NOT a perfect cube.
questions.hint: All prime factors must appear in multiples of 3

questions.q: Find the smallest number by which 192 must be divided to make it a perfect cube.
questions.ans: 192 = 2⁶×3. Factor 3 has no complete triple. Divide by 3: 192÷3=64=4³. Answer: 3.
questions.hint: Remove the prime that has no complete triple

questions.q: Why is 1729 called the Hardy-Ramanujan number?
questions.ans: 1729 is the smallest natural number expressible as the sum of two perfect cubes in two different ways: 1³+12³=1729 and 9³+10³=1729.
questions.hint: Two different ways to write as sum of two cubes

C.title: Section C — Short Answer II (3 marks each)

questions.q: Find the smallest number by which 8788 must be multiplied to make it a perfect cube. Find the cube root of the resulting number.
questions.ans: 8788 = 2²×13³. Factor 2 appears only twice. Multiply by 2: 8788×2=17576=2³×13³. ∛17576 = 2×13 = 26.
questions.hint: Find unpaired prime, multiply, then find cube root

questions.q: Show that ∛(m×n) = ∛m × ∛n using m=8 and n=27.
questions.ans: LHS: ∛(8×27) = ∛216 = 6. RHS: ∛8 × ∛27 = 2×3 = 6. LHS=RHS ✓. The property holds: cube root of a product equals the product of cube roots.
questions.hint: Compute both sides separately and compare

questions.q: A cubical tank holds 13824 litres of water. Find the length of its edge.

D.title: Section D — Long Answer (5 marks each)

questions.q: Find the smallest number by which 675 must be multiplied to make it a perfect cube. Also find the cube root of the resulting number.
questions.ans: 675 = 3³×5². Factor 5 appears only twice, needs one more 5. Multiply by 5: 675×5=3375=3³×5³=15³. ∛3375 = ∛(3³×5³) = 3×5 = 15.
questions.hint: Factorise, identify incomplete triple, multiply, then find cube root

questions.q: Verify that 1³+12³=9³+10³=1729. Why does this make 1729 special? Give one more example of a pair of cubes summing to 1729.
questions.ans: 1³=1, 12³=1728. 1+1728=1729 ✓. 9³=729, 10³=1000. 729+1000=1729 ✓. 1729 is special because it is the smallest positive integer that can be expressed as sum of two positive perfect cubes in TWO different ways. This was pointed out by Ramanujan to Hardy, making it the Hardy-Ramanujan number. Both pairs verified above.
questions.hint: Calculate each cube and verify; explain the significance
